Pressure ulcer stage codes.Pressure ulcer stages: Codes in L89 identify site and stage. Stages include 1–4, deep tissue pressure injury, unspecified stage, and unstageable. Assign as many codes as needed to identify all present ulcers. See Section I.B.14 for documentation by clinicians other than the patient's provider.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Unstageable pressure ulcers: Assign L89.--0 based on clinical documentation when the stage cannot be clinically determined (e.g., covered by eschar, treated with graft). Do not confuse with L89.--9 for unspecified stage. If stage is revealed after debridement, code only the revealed stage.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Documented pressure ulcer stage: Stage assignment should follow provider documentation or Alphabetic Index terms. If terms are absent and stage not documented, query the provider.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Patients admitted with pressure ulcers d: No code assigned if ulcer is completely healed at admission.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Pressure ulcers documented as healing: Assign stage code based on documentation. If stage not documented, assign unspecified stage code. Query if unclear whether ulcer is new or healing. If healed at discharge, code for site and stage at admission.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Patient admitted with pressure ulcer evo: Assign two codes: one for site/stage on admission and one for same site at highest stage during stay.
Pressure ulcer stage codes.Pressure-induced deep tissue damage: Assign only L89.--6 for pressure-induced deep tissue damage or deep tissue pressure injury.

Non-Pressure Chronic Ulcers.Patients admitted with non-pressure ulce: No code assigned if ulcer is completely healed at admission.
Non-Pressure Chronic Ulcers.Non-pressure ulcers documented as healin: Assign non-pressure ulcer code based on severity. If severity not documented, assign unspecified severity. Query if unclear whether ulcer is new or healing. If healed at discharge, code for site/severity at admission.
Non-Pressure Chronic Ulcers.Patient admitted with non-pressure ulcer: Assign two codes: one for site/severity on admission and one for same site at highest severity during stay.
Non-Pressure Chronic Ulcers.Reference: See Section I.B.14 for documentation by clinicians other than the patient's provider.
